  1. Krum, the Library /
  2. Tools /
  3. Miscellaneous Utilities

Miscellaneous Utilities¶

This module provides various helper functions and classes used throughout Krum for common operations like exception handling, parsing, timing, etc.

Key Functions and Classes¶

Exception Handling:

  • UnavailableException: Exception for missing entries in registries

  • fatal_unavailable: Helper to print and exit on unavailable entries

Registry Patterns:

  • MethodCallReplicator: Forward method calls to multiple instances

  • ClassRegister: Generic named class registry

Parsing:

  • parse_keyval: Parse key:value arguments from CLI

  • fullqual: Get fully qualified name of objects

Timing:

  • TimedContext: Measure execution time of code blocks

  • onetime: Thread-safe one-time flag

Utilities:

  • pairwise: Generate all pairs of indices

  • line_maximize: Find optimal attack factor

  • interactive: Simple interactive shell

Example

from tools import UnavailableException, parse_keyval, TimedContext

# Exception handling
try:
    raise UnavailableException({"a": 1, "b": 2}, "c", "option")
except UnavailableException as e:
    print(e)  # "Unknown option 'c', expected one of:

· a · b”

# Parse key:value arguments args = parse_keyval([“lr:0.01”, “batch:32”]) # {‘lr’: 0.01, ‘batch’: 32}

# Timing with TimedContext(“my_operation”):

# code here pass

class tools.misc.ClassRegister(singular, optplural=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Simple class register.

instantiate(name, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Instantiate a registered class. :param name Class name: :param … Forwarded parameters:

Returns:

Registered class instance

itemize()[source]¶

Build an iterable over the available class names. :Returns: Iterable over the available class names

register(name, cls)[source]¶

Register a new class. :param name Class name: :param cls Associated class:

class tools.misc.MethodCallReplicator(*args)[source]¶

Bases: object

Simple method call replicator class.

class tools.misc.TimedContext(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Context

Timed context class, that print the measure runtime.

exception tools.misc.UnavailableException(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: UserException

Exception due to missing entry in a dictionary, where the entry is controlled by the user.

tools.misc.deltatime_format(a, b)[source]¶

Compute and format the time elapsed between two points in time. :param a Earlier point-in-time: :param b Later point-in-time:

Returns:

  • Elapsed time integer (in s)

  • Formatted elapsed time string (human-readable way)

tools.misc.deltatime_point()[source]¶

Take a point in time. :Returns: Opaque point-in-time

tools.misc.fatal_unavailable(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Helper forwarding the ‘UnavailableException’ explanatory string to ‘fatal’. :param … Forward: :type … Forward: keyword-

tools.misc.fullqual(obj)[source]¶

Get the fully qualified name of an object for debugging.

Parameters:

obj (object) – A class or instance.

Returns:

str – Fully qualified name, e.g., ‘tools.misc.fullqual’ or ‘instance of pathlib.Path’.

Example

>>> fullqual(str)
'builtins.str'
>>> fullqual(pathlib.Path("."))
'instance of pathlib.Path'
tools.misc.get_loaded_dependencies()[source]¶

List non-builtin, currently loaded root module names and metadata. :Returns: List of tuples (<root module name>, <version or ‘None’>, <0 – is standard, 1: is site-specific, 2: is local>)

Raises:

'RuntimeError' on unsupported platforms –

tools.misc.interactive(glbs=None, lcls=None, prompt='>>> ', cprmpt='... ')[source]¶

Switch to a simple interactive prompt, execute CTRL+D (or equivalent) to leave. :param glbs Globals dictionary to use: :param None to use caller’s globals: :param lcls Locals dictionary to use: :param None to use given globals or caller’s locals/globals: :param prompt Command prompt to display: :param cprmpt Command prompt to display when continuing a line:

tools.misc.line_maximize(scape, evals=16, start=0.0, delta=1.0, ratio=0.8)[source]¶

Best-effort arg-maximize a scape: ℝ⁺⟶ ℝ, by mere exploration. :param scape Function to best-effort arg-maximize: :param evals Maximum number of evaluations: :param must be a positive integer: :param start Initial x evaluated: :param must be a non-negative float: :param delta Initial step delta: :param must be a positive float: :param ratio Contraction ratio: :type ratio Contraction ratio: both excluded :param must be between 0.5 and 1.: :type must be between 0.5 and 1.: both excluded

Returns:

Best-effort maximizer x under the evaluation budget

tools.misc.localtime()[source]¶

Return the formatted local time. :Returns: Human-readable local time

tools.misc.onetime(name=None)[source]¶

Generate a one time-set (hidden) state variable getter and setter. :param name Optional name of the global: :param onetime variable to access:

Returns:

  • · (Threadsafe)

  • · (Threadsafe)

tools.misc.pairwise(data)[source]¶

Generate all pairs (x, y) from data where index(x) < index(y).

This is useful for computing pairwise distances in aggregation rules.

Parameters:

data (iterable) – Indexable data structure (list, tuple) containing elements.

Yields:

tuple – Pairs (data[i], data[j]) for all i < j.

Example

>>> list(pairwise([1, 2, 3]))
[(1, 2), (1, 3), (2, 3)]
>>> list(pairwise("ab"))
[('a', 'b')]
tools.misc.parse_keyval(list_keyval, defaults={})[source]¶

Parse list of “key:value” strings into a dictionary.

This is the main method for parsing CLI arguments like “–gar-args lr:0.01”.

Parameters:

  • list_keyval (list of str) – List of “key:value” strings to parse.

  • defaults (dict, optional) – Default key -> value mappings. Also used for type inference: if a key exists in defaults, its value type is used to convert the input string.

Returns:

dict – Parsed dictionary with typed values.

Raises:

tools.UserException – If a key appears twice or if type conversion fails.

Notes

  • For bool values in defaults: “0”, “n”, “false” (case-insensitive) are False

  • For other types: uses the type constructor directly

  • Auto-detection for unknown keys: tries int, float, bool, then string

Example

>>> parse_keyval(["lr:0.01", "batch:32"], defaults={"lr": 0.1})
{'lr': 0.01, 'batch': 32}
>>> parse_keyval(["debug:true", "workers:4"])
{'debug': True, 'workers': 4}
